audit_items.proof_trace: ber_strdup() (wrapping ber_strdup_x) at line 71 can return NULL on allocation failure, but the result is used without a NULL check at line 80 (ldap_pvt_hex_unescape(p)) and line 82 (fopen(p, "rb")), both of which dereference the pointer unconditionally. Proof fails: no enforcement mechanism guards against the NULL case.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker that can cause memory pressure while a client processes LDIF data containing a file:// URL reference (via ldif_parse_line → ldif_fetch_url → ldif_open_url) can trigger a NULL dereference crash (DoS) when ber_strdup fails and the NULL pointer is passed to ldap_pvt_hex_unescape or fopen.
